#+title: Task: Migrate library/data/modeline.json to MASD-style org model
#+description: modeline.json holds three editor modeline strings (sql, c++, cmake) consumed by generator.py to inject mode-lines into generated file headers. As with profiles.json and components.json it is a JSON file inside a library that is otherwise moving to literate org-mode source. Migrate it to an org-mode model using the Dogen masd.org hierarchical pattern (module > element > per-field attributes with :PROPERTIES: drawers) as the target representation; update generator.py to read from the org source; delete modeline.json.
#+type: task
#+level: s1
#+filetags: :codegen_org_model_migration:sprint_20:v0:
#+owner: marco
#+branch: feature/migrate-modeline-json-to-org
#+pr:

This page documents a [[id:31C868B9-306E-4282-BA8C-07E647021B34][task]] in the [[id:D85E2B30-8C64-46DF-819D-B50D2E70B73C][Codegen unified model — org-mode migration]] story. It captures the goal, current status, acceptance, and any notes or results.

* Goal

Model =library/data/modeline.json= as a MASD-style org hierarchy following the
pattern established in Dogen's =share/library/masd.org=. In that model, each
modeline group is an org module-level heading with stereotype =:modeline_group:=,
each modeline within the group is a child heading with stereotype =:modeline:=,
and each field of the modeline (=name=, editor settings) is a grandchild
=:attribute:= heading whose value is given by the =masd.codec.value= property in
its =:PROPERTIES:= drawer.

For OreStudio's three modelines (sql, c++, cmake), the natural shape is a single
modeline group containing three modeline elements, each with a =content= attribute
that carries the emacs mode-line string. This mirrors how Dogen models its
=masd.lsp= / =masd.emacs= / =masd.vim= groups.

Once the org model is authored, update =generator.py= (or add a small dedicated
loader) to read modelines from the org source and drop the JSON load. Delete
=modeline.json=.

* Acceptance

- =library/data/modeline.org= (or =library/modeline.org=) exists and models the three modelines as a MASD-style hierarchy consistent with the Dogen =masd.org= pattern.
- =generator.py= loads modelines from the org source; the JSON load path is removed.
- =library/data/modeline.json= is deleted.
- Generated file headers are byte-identical before and after — a full codegen run produces zero =git diff= against HEAD.

* Plan

(Implementation strategy. Written when work starts; key decisions
are distilled into the parent story's =* Decisions= at close, but the
plan itself stays — it is the historical record of what we did.)

* Notes

Reference: Dogen =share/library/masd.org= models modelines as:

#+begin_example
* ores.modelines :module:
:PROPERTIES:
:custom_id: ...
:END:

** ores.modelines.emacs :modeline_group:
:PROPERTIES:
:custom_id: ...
:END:

*** ores.modelines.emacs.cpp :modeline:
:PROPERTIES:
:custom_id: ...
:END:

**** content :attribute:
:PROPERTIES:
:masd.codec.value: mode: c++; tab-width: 4; indent-tabs-mode: nil; c-basic-offset: 4
:END:
#+end_example

The OreStudio equivalent would be a single =ores.modelines= module with one
group =ores.modelines.emacs= and three children: =sql=, =cpp=, =cmake=.
